task_completion.description: The agent opened Jitsi Meet and joined the specified emergency response room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to Jitsi Meet (http://localhost:8080) and the room name 'Incident-Response-CRIT001' is visible in the URL bar or the meeting header.

task_completion.description: The Lobby feature is enabled for the meeting room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Security options' dialog in the Jitsi Meet interface shows that the 'Enable lobby' toggle is turned on.

task_completion.description: A meeting password is set for the room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Security options' dialog shows that a password has been entered and saved in the password field.

task_completion.description: The policy for all participants to start with their microphone muted is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jitsi settings menu (typically under the 'More' or 'Moderator' tab) shows that the 'Everyone starts muted' option is checked.

task_completion.description: The required incident response message is sent in the meeting chat.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jitsi chat panel is open and displays the exact message: 'INCIDENT RESPONSE ACTIVE - All responders acknowledge attendance'.

task_completion.description: The setup is documented in the required report file on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the file '/home/ga/Desktop/incident_response_meeting_report.txt' containing the meeting URL, activated security controls, the chat message, and access instructions.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with Jitsi Meet.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a graphical web browser open with the Jitsi Meet interface, rather than terminal commands making API/HTTP requests to configure the room.

integrity.description: The chat message was sent through the Jitsi GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The chat panel in the browser shows the message as sent by the local user, rather than being injected via browser developer tools or external scripts.

integrity.description: The agent created the report file using standard tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report is seen being edited in a text editor (like gedit, nano) or constructed via standard terminal commands, rather than copied from a pre-existing hidden file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Jitsi Meet instance is hosted at 'http://localhost:8080'. The required room name is exactly 'Incident-Response-CRIT001'. The exact chat message to be sent is 'INCIDENT RESPONSE ACTIVE - All responders acknowledge attendance'. The final report must be saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/incident_response_meeting_report.txt'.
